title: BlockQaReport
description: JSON shape of a per-content-block `<block>.qa.json` QA sidecar. Each content block (a .ts + .md + optional .lean triple) may carry one sidecar recording, per QA criterion, an append-only array of reviewer verdicts pinned to the content hashes they judged. Canonical interchange reference for the QOU block-QA format — readable from any language.

$schema.description: Schema marker for this sidecar format.

kind.description: Block kind — mirror of the .ts manifest's discriminator (definition / theorem / lemma / proposition / corollary / conjecture / example / remark / proof / simulator / prose / equation / diagram / table / algorithm).

ts.description: Manifest path, relative to repo root.

paths.description: Paths to the block's source files, relative to repo root.

source_hashes.description: Hashes of the present source files, refreshed on every write.

criteria.description: Per-criterion reviewer entries, keyed by criterion id (e.g. 'voice-status-leak', 'detangler-block-tanglement'). Multiple reviewers (script + agent + human) may co-exist; entries are NOT deduplicated. The criterion's current verdict is the most recent entry whose field_hash matches the present source files; if none matches, the criterion is stale and requires re-audit.

fieldHash.description: 12-char SHA-256 prefixes of each source file's UTF-8 bytes. Absent fields mean the audit did not depend on that file (or it did not exist at audit time).

kind.description: script = deterministic CLI checker (reproducible); agent = LLM-driven reviewer (not deterministic); human = repo owner / collaborator adjudication (final authority).

id.description: Stable identifier — script path, agent/skill name, or GitHub login.

version.description: Optional version pin (script revision, model id).

script_hash.description: 12-char SHA-256 prefix of the checker's own source file at audit time (kind: script). Mismatch with the current file means the checker logic changed and the entry is stale.

script_commit_sha.description: Full git SHA of the most recent commit touching the checker's source file at audit time. Audit trail only — staleness is driven by script_hash.

deps_hash.description: 12-char SHA-256 prefix of the concatenated extra inputs the checker consulted beyond the block under audit (e.g. the bibliography database). Mismatch means an input changed and the entry is stale.

agent_session.description: Reviewing agent's session id, if available.

agent_date.description: ISO date of the agent review.

agent_skill.description: Skill that dispatched the review, e.g. 'local/one-voice-audit', 'local/devils-advocate-watcher'.

reviewer.description: Identity + provenance of the reviewer that produced an entry.

score.description: Rubric score for rater-style quality criteria (e.g. proof-rater-*). A quality measure to improve over time, not a pass/fail gate. Convention: value/max >= 0.66 maps to result 'pass', 0.33-0.66 to 'warn', < 0.33 to 'fail'.

field_hash.description: Source-file hashes captured at audit time. A verdict whose field_hash no longer matches the present sources is stale and must be re-run.

result.description: pass = satisfied; fail = violated (evidence must cite file:line + verbatim quote); warn = borderline, flagged but not blocking; n/a = criterion does not apply to this block kind.

evidence.description: Evidence for fail/warn verdicts: either a free-form 'file:line + verbatim quote' string, or a structured list of {line, text} locations (the shape some agent reviewers emit, e.g. on the voice axis).

metrics.description: Optional structured numeric/heuristic measures the checker emits alongside its verdict — e.g. the detangler axis records per-block dependency-graph measures (fwd_emitted, tanglement_score, out_degree, in_degree, cone_size, depth, pagerank, edge_span_max, edge_span_mean, graph_energy, worst_target). Descriptive snapshots, not quality scores.

reviewed_at.description: When the audit ran. ISO-8601 UTC datetime; legacy agent entries may carry a bare ISO date.

reviewed_sha.description: Repo HEAD at audit time (full git SHA). Recommended; legacy agent entries (pre-2026-06) may omit it.

referee_argument.description: The objection stated in the referee's voice (1-3 sentences). On a da-referee-verdict rollup entry this holds the strongest objection.

rebuttal.description: What rebuts the objection, or 'none — survived because <why>'. For structural scope, must name the proved invariant.

allOf.description: result must agree with ruling on finding entries.
result must agree with verdict on the rollup entry.
structural scope requires a non-empty rebuttal naming the invariant.

criterionEntry.description: The outcome of one reviewer evaluating one criterion on one block.

daScope.description: Refutation scope per AGENTS.md 'Before declaring refuted'. 'limited' = this construction ALONE fails as stated (default). 'structural' = cannot hold in any closure; PERMITTED ONLY when referee_argument/rebuttal cite a proved invariant (parity/sign, dimension/degree/orbit-count bound, Lean-proved impossibility, entropy/precision bound).

daRuling.description: Adjudicator's verdict on a single objection after the block's own text/Lean/refs/corpus-grep were given the chance to rebut. surviving = no rebuttal found; rebutted = block defeats it; partial = block mitigates but does not fully defeat it.

daVerdict.description: Block-level rollup (criterion id da-referee-verdict). clean = all objections rebutted; survivable-objection = >=1 partial, 0 surviving; open-objection = >=1 surviving.
